//: PRD-046f f-AC-4 — the committed pull-through / redundant-search-reduction baseline the prime-eval gate enforces (baseline − ε, ε = EPSILON_PRIME in src/eval/prime.ts).
//placeholder: ADVISORY — NOT yet enforced. The gate computes the verdict + reasons but never FAILS a run while placeholder=true (same posture as PRD-027/045 started).
//measured-2026-06-22: Ran live twice against workspace=default (the only workspace this token can write — honeycomb_ci is 403). Directional result is SOLID: primed BEATS cold both runs (cold pull-through is structurally 0). BUT the magnitude is UNSTABLE: run1 pull-through=1.000 search-reduction=2.600; run2 pull-through=0.400 search-reduction=1.100 (and run0 failed at the convergence barrier — eventual-consistency flake). A 0.4↔1.0 swing is ~12× ε, so the earlier assumption ('ε absorbs workspace churn') is FALSE in a busy shared workspace. ROOT CAUSE: the prime digest is assembled from the WHOLE workspace, recency-ordered + token-bounded, so this run's seeded targets compete with all of `default`'s other memories (real + 36 hybrid-benchmark seeds + prior prime-eval seeds) — which of them survive the budget shuffles run-to-run. This is the SAME shared-workspace instability PRD-027's recall-eval fixed with per-run isolation / relevance-class scoring, which this newer 046f eval LACKS.
//to-enforce: Do NOT flip placeholder=false from a busy shared workspace. Either (a) run `npm run eval:prime` in the CI environment whose token authorizes the fresh per-run honeycomb_ci workspace (the digest then contains ONLY this run's seeds → stable ~1.0), confirm N consecutive stable runs, then write the measured values AT-OR-BELOW the stable number; OR (b) harden the 046f itest to score against a per-run-scoped digest (mirror recall-eval's two-phase barrier + relevance-class) so it is stable in any workspace, THEN flip. Tracked as follow-up `046f-prime-eval-isolation`.
